content:
# 1 Corinthians Chapter 12 — Disagreement Log
**Arbiter:** SOPHIA | **In council with:** EMET, LOGOS, PNEUMA
**Date:** 2026-03-05

This log records all verses in 1 Corinthians Chapter 12 where the three translators (EMET, LOGOS, PNEUMA) significantly diverged in their renderings, along with SOPHIA's resolution and reasoning.

---

## 12:1 — "spiritual gifts" / "the spiritual [things]" / "the special abilities the Spirit gives"

| Translator | Rendering | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "concerning the spiritual [things]" | Literal ton pneumatikon — no noun supplied |
| LOGOS | "concerning spiritual gifts" | Supplies "gifts" (charismata implied) |
| PNEUMA | "regarding your question about the special abilities the Spirit gives us" | Full dynamic expansion |

**SOPHIA's resolution:** Adopted "spiritual gifts" (LOGOS). The Greek ton pneumatikon is ambiguous — it could mean "spiritual things," "spiritual persons," or "spiritual gifts." Context (the gift lists that follow) supports "gifts." PNEUMA's "special abilities" narrows the concept to abilities, whereas charismata includes broader realities (faith, helps, administration). PNEUMA also adds "your question about" (not in the text).

**Status:** Resolved in favor of LOGOS.

---

## 12:2 — "pagans" / "nations" / "still pagans"

| Translator | Rendering | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "when you were nations" | Literal ethne |
| LOGOS | "when you were pagans" | Interprets ethne in religious context |
| PNEUMA | "when you were still pagans" | Adds "still" |

**SOPHIA's resolution:** Adopted "pagans" (LOGOS). EMET's "nations" is literal but in English does not convey the religious connotation. PNEUMA's "still" implies a lingering condition not in the text.

**Status:** Resolved in favor of LOGOS.

---

## 12:2 — "mute idols" / "speechless idols" / "speechless idols"

| Translator | Rendering | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "the mute idols" | Literal aphona — "voiceless/mute" |
| LOGOS | "mute idols" | Same, without article |
| PNEUMA | "speechless idols" | "Speechless" — slightly different register |

**SOPHIA's resolution:** Adopted "mute idols" (LOGOS). Minor variation; "mute" is more common and forceful in this context than "speechless."

**Status:** Resolved in favor of LOGOS. Minor variation.

---

## 12:3 — "Jesus is accursed" / "Yeshua is accursed" — name rendering

| Translator | Rendering | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "Yeshua is accursed...Yeshua is Lord" | Hebrew name form |
| LOGOS | "Jesus is accursed...Jesus is Lord" | Standard English |
| PNEUMA | "Jesus is accursed...Jesus is Lord" | Standard English |

**SOPHIA's resolution:** Adopted "Jesus" (LOGOS/PNEUMA). Consistent name-rendering policy throughout the consensus text.

**Status:** Resolved in favor of LOGOS/PNEUMA.

---

## 12:4-6 — "gifts/service/activities" vs. "gifts/ministries/workings" vs. "gifts/service/ways"

| Translator | Rendering | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "diversities of gifts...diversities of ministries...diversities of workings" | Literal charismaton/diakonion/energematon |
| LOGOS | "varieties of gifts...varieties of service...varieties of activities" | Formal equivalent |
| PNEUMA | "different kinds of spiritual gifts...different kinds of service...God works in different ways" | Dynamic with structural variation |

**SOPHIA's resolution:** Adopted LOGOS's rendering. The parallel structure (varieties of X...varieties of Y...varieties of Z) must be preserved because Paul uses it to mirror the trinitarian pattern (Spirit/Lord/God). PNEUMA breaks the parallelism in v.6 by restructuring the sentence. EMET's "ministries" and "workings" are slightly more technical than needed.

**Status:** Resolved in favor of LOGOS. Parallel trinitarian structure preserved.

---

## 12:7 — "for the common good" / "for the benefit [of all]" / "so we can help each other"

| Translator | Rendering | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "for the benefit [of all]" | Literal pros to sympheron with bracket |
| LOGOS | "for the common good" | Established rendering |
| PNEUMA | "so we can help each other" | Dynamic simplification |

**SOPHIA's resolution:** Adopted "for the common good" (LOGOS). This is the established rendering of pros to sympheron and captures Paul's communal emphasis. PNEUMA's "help each other" is reductive — the "common good" includes more than mutual assistance.

**Status:** Resolved in favor of LOGOS.

---

## 12:8-10 — "utterance of wisdom" / "word of wisdom" / "ability to give wise advice"

| Translator | Rendering | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "[the] word of wisdom...word of knowledge" | Literal logos sophias/gnoseos |
| LOGOS | "the utterance of wisdom...the utterance of knowledge" | "Utterance" for logos |
| PNEUMA | "the ability to give wise advice...a message of special knowledge" | Dynamic expansion |

**SOPHIA's resolution:** Adopted "utterance" (LOGOS). "Word" (EMET) is literal but ambiguous in English (could mean a single word). "Utterance" captures the communicative act. PNEUMA's "ability to give wise advice" shifts from a Spirit-given communication to a human skill.

**Status:** Resolved in favor of LOGOS.

---

## 12:10 — "the working of miracles" / "workings of powers" / "the power to perform miracles"

| Translator | Rendering | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "workings of powers" | Literal energemata dynameon |
| LOGOS | "the working of miracles" | Standard rendering |
| PNEUMA | "the power to perform miracles" | Restructured as ability |

**SOPHIA's resolution:** Adopted "the working of miracles" (LOGOS). EMET's "powers" for dynameis is literal but opaque. PNEUMA's restructuring shifts from God's working to human ability.

**Status:** Resolved in favor of LOGOS.

---

## 12:10 — "distinguish between spirits" / "discernment of spirits" / "discern whether a message is from the Spirit of God or from another spirit"

| Translator | Rendering | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "discernment of spirits" | Literal diakriseis pneumaton |
| LOGOS | "the ability to distinguish between spirits" | Expanded for clarity |
| PNEUMA | "the ability to discern whether a message is from the Spirit of God or from another spirit" | Full explanatory expansion |

**SOPHIA's resolution:** Adopted LOGOS's rendering. PNEUMA's expansion specifies what the discernment involves, which is interpretive commentary rather than translation.

**Status:** Resolved in favor of LOGOS.

---

## 12:12 — "so it is with Christ" / "so also [is] the Christos" / "So it is with the body of Christ"

| Translator | Rendering | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "so also [is] the Christos" | Literal — preserves Paul's unexpected "the Christ" rather than "the church" |
| LOGOS | "so it is with Christ" | Smooth English preserving the christological reference |
| PNEUMA | "So it is with the body of Christ" | Adds "body of" — interpretive clarification |

**SOPHIA's resolution:** Adopted LOGOS's "so it is with Christ." Paul deliberately writes "the Christ" (ho Christos), not "the church." PNEUMA's addition of "body of" softens Paul's striking identification. The point is that the church IS Christ, not merely Christ's body — a distinction Paul makes intentionally.

**Status:** Resolved in favor of LOGOS. Paul's bold christological identification preserved.

---

## 12:13 — "Jews or Greeks, slaves or free" / "Ioudaioi or Greeks" / "Jews...Gentiles...slaves...free"

| Translator | Rendering | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "whether Ioudaioi or Greeks, whether slaves or free" | Transliterates Ioudaioi |
| LOGOS | "Jews or Greeks, slaves or free" | Standard translation |
| PNEUMA | "Some of us are Jews, some are Gentiles, some are slaves, and some are free" | Expands pairs into four clauses; substitutes "Gentiles" |

**SOPHIA's resolution:** Adopted LOGOS's rendering. PNEUMA replaces "Greeks" with "Gentiles" (Paul writes Hellenes) and restructures the binary pairs into a four-part list, losing the deliberate pairing structure.

**Status:** Resolved in favor of LOGOS.

---

## 12:13 — "made to drink of one Spirit" / "given to drink one Spirit" / "we all share the same Spirit"

| Translator | Rendering | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "we all were given to drink one Spirit" | Literal epotisthemen — "were made to drink" |
| LOGOS | "all were made to drink of one Spirit" | Formal equivalent preserving the drink metaphor |
| PNEUMA | "we all share the same Spirit" | Drops the drink metaphor entirely |

**SOPHIA's resolution:** Adopted LOGOS's rendering. The drink/watering metaphor (epotisthemen) is theologically significant — it connects to baptismal imagery and the water-from-the-rock typology of 10:4. PNEUMA's "share" loses this concrete image.

**Status:** Resolved in favor of LOGOS. The drink metaphor is preserved.

---

## 12:22 — "indispensable" / "necessary" / "the most necessary"

| Translator | Rendering | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "the members of the body seeming to be weaker are necessary" | Literal anankaia |
| LOGOS | "the parts of the body that seem to be weaker are indispensable" | "Indispensable" — stronger term |
| PNEUMA | "some parts of the body that seem weakest and least important are actually the most necessary" | Adds "least important" and superlative "most" |

**SOPHIA's resolution:** Adopted "indispensable" (LOGOS). This is stronger than "necessary" (EMET) and captures the force of Paul's inversion. PNEUMA's additions ("least important," "most necessary") amplify the contrast beyond what the Greek states.

**Status:** Resolved in favor of LOGOS.

---

## 12:28 — "helping, administrating" / "helps, administrations" / "those who can help others, those who have the gift of leadership"

| Translator | Rendering | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "helps, administrations" | Literal antilempseis, kyberneseis |
| LOGOS | "helping, administrating" | Gerund forms |
| PNEUMA | "those who can help others, those who have the gift of leadership" | Expands into full clauses; renders kyberneseis as "leadership" |

**SOPHIA's resolution:** Adopted LOGOS's rendering. PNEUMA's "leadership" for kyberneseis is a common but imprecise rendering — the term means "steering/piloting/governing" (from kubernetes, "helmsman"), emphasizing practical guidance rather than hierarchical authority. "Administrating" better captures the navigational metaphor.

**Status:** Resolved in favor of LOGOS.

---

## 12:31 — "a still more excellent way" / "a way beyond all comparison" / "a way of life that is best of all"

| Translator | Rendering | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "a way beyond all comparison" | Intensifies kath' hyperbolen |
| LOGOS | "a still more excellent way" | Traditional rendering |
| PNEUMA | "a way of life that is best of all" | Interprets "way" as "way of life" |

**SOPHIA's resolution:** Adopted LOGOS's "a still more excellent way." This is the traditional and recognizable rendering. EMET's "beyond all comparison" is a legitimate rendering of kath' hyperbolen but over-translates. PNEUMA's "way of life" adds a specificity not in hodon ("way/road/path").

**Status:** Resolved in favor of LOGOS.

---

## Summary of Disagreement Patterns

| Pattern | Count | Description |
|---|---|---|
| PNEUMA interpretive expansion | 8 | Added explanatory clauses, replaced terms with interpretive glosses |
| EMET transliteration/literalism | 4 | Ioudaioi, Christos, technical terms |
| Theological precision | 4 | "Common good," "indispensable," "participation in Christ," gift terminology |
| Structural preservation | 2 | Trinitarian parallel (vv.4-6), binary pair structure (v.13) |
| Christological identification | 1 | "So it is with Christ" — preserving Paul's identification of church as Christ |

**Overall pattern:** Chapter 12 required careful attention to Paul's deliberate structural choices — the trinitarian parallel in vv.4-6, the binary social pairs in v.13, and the startling christological identification in v.12. In each case, PNEUMA's dynamic renderings tended to obscure these structures, while LOGOS preserved them. The gift terminology produced consistent three-way divergence, with EMET retaining technical Greek-derived terms, LOGOS providing formal equivalents, and PNEUMA offering functional descriptions.

---

*— SOPHIA, AI Translator, in council with EMET, LOGOS, and PNEUMA*
